title: Yara and the Scared Little Bird
synopsis: When Yara finds a scared baby bird that has fallen from its nest, she learns about kindness and helping others. With patience and gentle care, Yara helps the little bird feel safe and finds a way to reunite it with its family, discovering that being helpful makes her feel happy inside.

pages.text: Yara loved playing in her backyard garden. She had dark curly hair and wore her favorite yellow dress with sunflowers on it. Today she was looking at the pretty red roses when she heard a tiny sound. 'Peep! Peep!' What could it be?

illustration.description: A young girl with dark curly hair in a bright yellow sundress with sunflower patterns, kneeling beside vibrant red roses in a lush garden. Her expression shows curiosity as she tilts her head to listen. The background has green grass and colorful flowers with a textured, construction paper-like quality.
illustration.alt: Yara in yellow dress listening curiously in a colorful garden

pages.text: Behind the big rose bush, Yara found a tiny baby bird. It was gray and fluffy, and it looked very scared. Its little eyes were wide and it was shaking. 'Oh no, little bird,' said Yara softly. 'You must have fallen from your nest. Don't be scared.'

illustration.description: Yara crouched down looking at a small, fluffy gray baby bird on the green grass near rose bushes. The bird appears frightened with wide eyes and ruffled feathers. Yara's face shows concern and gentleness. The scene has warm, soft lighting with rich greens and reds from the garden plants.
illustration.alt: Yara discovering a scared baby bird on the ground

pages.text: Yara felt sad when she saw how scared the little bird was. She wanted to help, but she didn't know how. The bird was too small to fly back to its nest in the tall oak tree. 'Don't worry, little friend,' Yara whispered. 'I will find a way to help you.'

illustration.description: Yara sitting cross-legged on the grass next to the baby bird, with a tall oak tree visible in the background. Her expression shows determination mixed with concern. The baby bird sits nearby, still looking small and vulnerable. Dappled sunlight filters through tree leaves, creating a gentle, hopeful atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Yara promising to help the scared baby bird

pages.text: Yara ran inside to ask her mama for help. Her mama had kind brown eyes just like Yara's. 'We need to be very gentle,' Mama said. 'Baby birds are fragile.' Together, they made a soft nest from an old towel in a small basket.

illustration.description: Yara and her mother, a woman with warm brown skin and long dark hair in a kitchen, preparing a makeshift nest. They're placing a soft blue towel in a woven basket. Both have gentle, caring expressions. The kitchen has bright yellow walls and colorful details, creating a warm, nurturing environment.
illustration.alt: Yara and her mama preparing a soft nest for the baby bird

pages.text: Very carefully, Yara picked up the baby bird with the soft towel. She moved slowly so she wouldn't scare it more. The little bird stopped shaking when it felt the warm, cozy towel. 'There you go, little one,' Yara said with a happy smile.

illustration.description: Yara gently holding the baby bird wrapped in a soft blue towel, with a tender smile on her face. The bird looks calmer now, nestled safely in the towel. The scene is bathed in warm golden light, emphasizing the gentleness and care. Yara's expression shows joy at helping the small creature.
illustration.alt: Yara gently holding the baby bird in a soft towel

pages.text: Yara put the bird in the cozy basket and placed it in a quiet spot in the garden. She felt proud and happy that she was helping. The baby bird looked up at her with its bright little eyes and made a soft, happy peep. It wasn't scared anymore!

illustration.description: The baby bird sitting contentedly in the basket lined with the blue towel, placed on a garden table surrounded by colorful flowers. Yara stands nearby with a proud, joyful expression, hands clasped together. The bird appears much calmer, with bright alert eyes looking up trustingly.
illustration.alt: The calm baby bird in its cozy basket with happy Yara nearby

pages.text: Soon, Yara heard chirping from the oak tree. 'Chirp! Chirp!' It was the mama bird and papa bird! They had been looking everywhere for their baby. They flew down and perched on the basket, so excited to see their little one was safe.

illustration.description: Two adult birds, one brown and one with blue markings, perched on the edge of the basket containing the baby bird. Their wings are slightly spread with joy and relief. Yara watches with delight in the background. The oak tree looms above them, and the scene is filled with warm, golden afternoon light.
illustration.alt: Parent birds reuniting with their baby bird while Yara watches happily

pages.text: The mama and papa bird gently helped their baby climb onto papa's back. The little family was together again! Before they flew away, the baby bird looked at Yara and gave one more happy peep, as if to say 'Thank you!' Yara felt so happy and proud.

illustration.description: The baby bird perched on its papa's back while mama bird flies nearby, all three birds positioned as if ready to take flight. Yara waves goodbye with a radiant smile, her yellow dress bright against the green garden. The scene captures a moment of joy and successful reunion with warm, celebratory colors.
illustration.alt: The bird family reunited and ready to fly away as Yara waves goodbye

pages.text: That night, Yara told her mama all about her brave day. 'You were very kind to help the scared little bird,' Mama said, giving Yara a big hug. Yara went to sleep feeling happy, knowing that helping others made her heart feel warm and good.

illustration.description: Yara tucked into bed with soft purple blankets, smiling as her mama sits beside her. Through the bedroom window, the oak tree is visible in the moonlight. The room has a cozy, peaceful atmosphere with warm lamp light. Both Yara and her mama have content, loving expressions.
illustration.alt: Yara in bed with her mama, feeling happy about helping the bird

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Yara feel when she first saw the scared baby bird?
questions.explanation: Yara felt sad when she saw how scared the little bird was, which made her want to help.

questions.question: What did Yara and her mama use to make a soft nest for the bird?
questions.explanation: Yara and her mama made a soft nest from an old towel in a small basket.

questions.question: How did the baby bird feel after Yara helped it?
questions.explanation: The baby bird felt happy and safe - it stopped shaking and wasn't scared anymore.

questions.question: How did helping the bird make Yara feel?
questions.explanation: Helping the bird made Yara feel happy and proud, with a warm feeling in her heart.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Yara found a tiny baby [0] that was very [1]. She wanted to [2] it feel better.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Yara made a [0] nest with a soft [1]. The baby bird felt [2] and warm.
